title: Analyst Assurance Desk

fields.vision: An analyst-assurance workstation for human review of AI-supported intelligence tasks.
fields.mechanism: It makes override decisions, analyst-model disagreement, task-family calibration, source review, structured analytic technique outputs, uncertainty, assumptions, reasoning paths, scoring rules, and release chronology visible as native objects.
fields.built_components: Visible public components: 96 kept Evidence Sections, native navigation, Review Notes, source-use plan, APA lineage, and source lineage claim limit. The public package remains text-focused and does not claim unverified runtime state.
fields.source_utilization: analytic standards icd 203. Source family appears in the page source plan with 14 linked or queued rows; final citation strength still depends on passage-level review. nist ai risk management. Source family appears in the page source plan with 13 linked or queued rows; final citation strength still depends on passage-level review. structured analytic techniques. Source family appears in the page source plan with 13 linked or queued rows; final citation strength still depends on passage-level review. ai governance law policy. Source family appears in the page source plan with 1 linked or queued rows; final citation strength still depends on passage-level review. ai incident and responsibility literature. Source family appears in the page source plan with 1 linked or queued rows; final citation strength still depends on passage-level review. A page-family source-use record exists. Final citation strength still depends on page-specific passage review.
fields.plan: Plan: separate prototype capability from validated finding, bind claims to test materials, expose false-positive and false-negative risks, and add method-specific source anchors. The project source notes below should be converted into authored subsections only after the page-specific claim limit and source-use decisions are settled.
fields.status: Status: native public port complete for upload and navigation; substantive source-project verification remains limited by the source lineage ceiling and the original project governance records. The page separates public presentation from project completion; completion depends on the status and remaining-gap fields.
fields.remaining_gaps: Remaining gaps: many AI pages need stronger page-specific experimental status, clearer validation boundaries, and less reliance on representative method bibliography. The kept Evidence Sections below preserve project substance for close reading; final public prose should be authored from those signals rather than copied mechanically.
fields.detailed_source_vision: Analyst Assurance Desk is presented as an AI assurance and retrieval-integrity instrument. Its public purpose is to make AI system failure, retrieval integrity, analyst oversight, and model-governance problems legible as inspectable professional instruments. Source lead: The Analyst Assurance Desk becomes more inspectable when it shows override decisions, analyst-model disagreement, task-family calibration, and release chronology as native objects instead of leaving them implicit in the workflow prose.
fields.detailed_source_mechanism: The working mechanism is to move from threat model to test condition, system behavior, error mode, human-review checkpoint, scoring rule, and release or rejection gate. The reader should be able to ask what enters the room, what transformation occurs, what judgment or work product leaves, and which gate prevents overclaiming.

source_derived_signals.text: Kept human authority should not remain a slogan. It should tell the reader what kinds of model output trigger analyst intervention and why that override exists.

This room pairs the model-assessment and failure-taxonomy pages. Together they turn the usual diffuse discussion of AI risk into an inspection surface: what can fail, how severely it matters, how the model is scored, and what a human reviewer must still verify.
Source, method, confidence, and release overrides are separated rather than collapsed.

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Vision: This document supports the public account of Blueprint by preserving the project purpose, reader-facing claim, and professional-portfolio role.
public_standard_check.Mechanism: It connects source material, project components, implementation state, and next actions so the work can be inspected rather than merely asserted.
public_standard_check.What is built: The supporting evidence is the named page, blueprint, packet, project file, source record, or implementation page described in the document.
public_standard_check.Source use: Sources are used as method and source background, domain grounding, or project history; direct quotation and stronger factual claims require passage-level review.
public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.
public_standard_check.Status: Current support record for the professional package unless explicitly marked archival.
public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.

public_pitch.what_it_is: An analyst-assurance workstation for human review of AI-supported intelligence tasks.
public_pitch.distinctive_mechanism: It makes override decisions, analyst-model disagreement, task-family calibration, source review, structured analytic technique outputs, uncertainty, assumptions, reasoning paths, scoring rules, and release chronology visible as native objects.
public_pitch.why_it_matters: It is meant to show what human-in-the-loop review actually requires: not trust in a reviewer, but surfaces that let the reviewer inspect sources, assumptions, uncertainty, reasoning paths, and machine-aided judgments before release.
public_pitch.pitch_integrity_note: Mechanism-first public pitch inserted after the Nexus Lab mechanism-erasure audit on 2026-05-13; source basis: rendered correction table.

pitch_integrity_audit.standard: object, distinctive mechanism, value, built state, and non-overclaim discipline must be visible.

researcher_process_map.summary: An analyst-assurance workstation for human review of AI-supported intelligence tasks.
researcher_process_map.final_use_case: It is meant to show what human-in-the-loop review actually requires: not trust in a reviewer, but surfaces that let the reviewer inspect sources, assumptions, uncertainty, reasoning paths, and machine-aided judgments before release.

instruments.input: intelligence or research question, decision context, evidence type, uncertainty level, and user role
instruments.operation: selects the structured analytic technique or technique sequence that fits the task rather than applying SAT labels ornamentally
instruments.output: method assignment and technique rationale as a reviewable artifact
instruments.upstream_dependency: Requires intelligence or research question, decision context, evidence type, uncertainty level, and user role
instruments.downstream_use: Feeds method assignment and technique rationale as a reviewable artifact; later claims should not rely on this step until its expected output is present.
instruments.proof: Expected proof artifact: method assignment and technique rationale as a reviewable artifact
instruments.status: Status: native public port complete for upload and navigation; substantive source-project verification remains limited by the source lineage ceiling and the.
instruments.gap: Verify one representative case: the step should accept intelligence or research question, decision context, evidence type, uncertainty level, and user role and produce method assignment and technique rationale as a.
instruments.verification_check: Trace one Russia claim through Task And Method Assignment; confirm source lane, analytic variable, confidence boundary, and policy-facing output.
